mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a rank IC above 0.04 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during post-crisis recovery phases indicates capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative or flat 20-day forward returns during post-crisis recovery periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5 percent eliminates real yield drag on equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and total nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, lifting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: The combined positive momentum of GDPC1 and PAYEMS yields a negative rank IC over 60-day horizons in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a declining unemployment rate signals peak labor demand and wage pressure that compresses corporate margins, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Declining UNRATE produces positive 20-day forward returns in late-cycle expansions with a statistical significance level above 90 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side momentum that translates into positive short-term equity pricing over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns with a win rate above 52 percent over a 5-year sample.
